seoTitle: Camp Creek vs Campbelltown — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Camp Creek and Campbelltown. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Camp Creek vs Campbelltown: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Camp Creek and Campbelltown.
heading: Camp Creek vs Campbelltown — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Camp Creek, New South Wales and Campbelltown, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Camp Creek and Campbelltown have similar overall climate risk scores.

faq.question: Is Camp Creek or Campbelltown safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Camp Creek has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Campbelltown scores 23/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Camp Creek scores 59/100 for bushfire risk. Campbelltown scores 48/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Camp Creek or Campbelltown?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Camp Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.35% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
